From the historic Ditmas Park Victorian homes to the bustling commercial corridors along Flatbush Avenue and Church Avenue, our team knows how to protect every type of structure in this vibrant Brooklyn neighborhood.

faqs.question: How much does carpenter ant control cost in Flatbush?
faqs.answer: Carpenter ant control in Flatbush typically ranges from $350 to $2,000, depending on the severity of the infestation, property size, and treatment method required. Larger homes or multi-unit buildings with extensive colonies may require more comprehensive treatment. We provide free inspections and detailed quotes before beginning any work.

faqs.question: How can I tell if I have carpenter ants versus regular ants in my Flatbush home?
faqs.answer: Carpenter ants are significantly larger than common ants (up to ½ inch long) and are typically black or dark brown. Key signs include piles of sawdust-like wood shavings near baseboards or windows, rustling sounds inside walls, and winged ants emerging indoors during spring. Unlike termites, carpenter ants don't eat wood but excavate it to build nests, leaving smooth galleries.

faqs.question: Why are carpenter ants so common in Flatbush?
faqs.answer: Flatbush's abundance of mature trees, older wooden structures, and homes with wood siding create perfect habitats for carpenter ants. The neighborhood's pre-war buildings often have moisture issues from aging plumbing or roof leaks, which attract these pests to damp wood. Our tree-lined streets also provide easy access routes from outdoor colonies into homes.

faqs.question: How long does carpenter ant treatment take to work in Flatbush homes?
faqs.answer: Most customers notice significant reduction in carpenter ant activity within 7-10 days of initial treatment. Complete colony elimination typically takes 2-4 weeks, as treatments must reach the queen and satellite colonies. We often schedule follow-up visits to ensure all nests are destroyed and provide monitoring to prevent re-infestation, especially important in Flatbush's densely built environment.
